title: artemisia arbuscula subsp. thermopola
intro: Artemisia arbuscula subsp. thermopola, also known as dwarf wormwood, is a subspecies of the artemisia arbuscula plant native to the high mountains of Europe. It is a shrubby perennial herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the artemisia arbuscula subsp. thermopola plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
